rulings.Case Title: GILBERT FORBES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 3, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Union Carbide Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is continued to May 10, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 220 per the stipulation and order signed April 28. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELO DATIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 3, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant W.W. Grainger, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product supplied or sold by defendant. Defendant satisfied its burden of production in two ways: 1) by negating an element of plaintiff¿s case through the declarations of Mr. Fondren and Mr. Bahnmaier and 2) by presenting factually devoid discovery responses of the plaintiff. Plaintiff¿s objections to the declarations of Mr. Fondren and Mr. Bahnmaier are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to the declaration of Mr. Ay are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELO DATIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 3, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant United Refrigeration, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are continued to May 12, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 220 per agreement of the parties. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS HIXSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 3, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Collins Electrical Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing fireproofing disturbed by defendant. Except for the objection to the portion of plaintiff¿s declaration where states that plaintiff saw the installation of fireproofing at the SFO and Macy¿s job sites, defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s request for sanctions is denied. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS BOICOURT VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 3, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Bayer Cropscience, Inc. successor to AMCHEM¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are continued on the court¿s own motion to May 18, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 220 to allow defendant to file supplemental reply papers solely as to Mr. Baker¿s declaration and deposition testimony. Defendant¿s supplemental reply papers, if any, must be filed and served by May 12. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES E. OWENS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 3, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Gardena Holdings, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the deposition testimony of Mr. Dye, Mr. Turnage and Mr. Beckley are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES E. OWENS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 3, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Henkel Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication of punitive damages is off calendar. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. By failing to attach plaintiff¿s responses to specially prepared contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant cannot successfully invoke the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff¿s objections to Mr. Lehmann¿s declaration are sustained. Plaintiff dismissed his claim for punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES E. OWENS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 3, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Aerojet-General Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff failed to produce admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to any asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the portions of plaintiff¿s declaration regarding products attributable to defendant other than adhesive products are sustained on the basis of the D¿Amico rule. Defendant¿s objection to exhibit 2 to Ms. Meyer¿s declaration is sustained on grounds of lack of authentication. = (220/HEK)
